mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, consumer spending collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signal a synchronized collapse in aggregate demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 45 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates robust economic capacity that outpaces inflation concerns, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening cycles yield negative or flat 5-day forward returns in over 60 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 1.0 percent indicates exhausted monetary policy space and heightened recession risk, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when FEDFUNDS drops below 1.0 percent, with a t-statistic above 1.5 for positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and falling total nonfarm payrolls signals severe labor market deterioration, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The UNRATE-PAYEMS divergence shows no predictive power for 60-day returns, with a rank correlation coefficient below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real GDP growth directly boost corporate revenue expectations and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates exhibit zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 2017 baseline index of 100 signals severe capacity utilization drops and recessionary conditions, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of periods where INDPRO trades below 100.
